name: MOUNT & BLADE 2: BANNERLORD.
description:
Mount & Blade II: Bannerlord, the continent of Calradia is depicted as a sprawling land in the midst of a "Great Decline," roughly 200 years before the events of the previous game, Warband. The overarching lore centers on the crumbling Calradic Empire, a once-unrivaled superpower modeled after the Roman Empire, which has fallen into a state of chaotic disintegration following the death of Emperor Arenicos. With no clear heir and competing visions for the future—ranging from republicanism to hereditary rule—the land has been carved up by internal usurpers and opportunist external kingdoms. This era, known as the Interregnum, sets the stage for a massive continental war where the player must choose to either restore the Empire to its former glory or watch it burn under the heels of rising barbarian and nomadic powers.

The Calradic Empire (Northern, Southern, and Western)

The Empire is the historical heart of the continent, now split into three rival factions following a bitter succession crisis. The Northern Empire represents the traditionalists who believe the Senate should choose the leader; the Southern Empire is led by Empress Rhagaea, who seeks to establish a dynasty for her daughter; and the Western Empire follows Garios, a populist general who believes the legions themselves should crown their leader. Culturally, these factions share the Empire legacy, which provides a 20% reduction in garrison wages and a 25% increase in influence gained from being in an army. This reflects their highly organized, bureaucratic infrastructure and the disciplined nature of their military logistics, allowing them to maintain vast fortifications and large, state-sponsored war machines more effectively than their "barbarian" neighbors.

Militarily, the Empire is defined by versatility and heavy armor, serving as the game's premier "combined arms" force. Their strength lies in their ability to handle any tactical situation with top-tier units across all branches: the Imperial Legionary is a fortress of a soldier with high-quality shields and armor, while the Imperial Palatine Guard offers reliable, high-damage archery. However, their crowning jewel is the Elite Cataphract, a heavy shock cavalryman encased in iron from head to toe, capable of charging through almost any formation with impunity. While they lack the specialized "extreme" strengths of other cultures, their lack of a glaring weakness makes them the most adaptable and resilient force on the battlefield.

Lore-wise, the Empire is a tragic figure of fallen majesty, struggling to reconcile its sophisticated laws and ancient history with the brutal reality of its declining power. The landscape is dotted with ruined aqueducts and massive stone fortresses that serve as reminders of a unified past that may never return. The three sub-factions are locked in a "deadly triangle" of politics, where each leader views the others as illegitimate pretenders. For a player, the Empire offers a path of reconstruction, where one can navigate the complex web of senatorial intrigue and military coups to finally unite the eagle standards under a single, absolute ruler.

The Kingdom of Nordvyg (War Sails DLC)

The Kingdom of Nordvyg is the latest addition to Calradia, introduced in the War Sails expansion as a seafaring superpower from the distant "Nordlands." Historically, the Nords were known to Calradia only as coastal raiders and mercenaries, but under the leadership of King Sigurd, they have established permanent settlements in the newly expanded northern fjords. Their culture is centered entirely on the sea and the harsh life of the frozen coasts, giving them unique cultural buffs: they receive a 25% reduction in naval travel costs and their parties move 10% faster on water. Furtherm
creator: Nutscum
